bevy_constraints.bundle_strategy: Use bevy_ecs_tilemap or TextureAtlasLayout slices sourced from one tile sheet.
bevy_constraints.asset_loading: Load world/world_tower_tilekit_32px.png and register named rects for floors, ladders, door trims, scale anchors, and wall caps during Loading.
runtime_notes: Every collision-bearing top edge should map cleanly to 32 px world units so Rapier colliders match art without manual offsets.
Ladder and vine segments must stack seamlessly on a 32 px grid.
Keep decorative gold linework one material step brighter than the background so interactive anchors are easy to identify.

context.usage: Reusable platform, wall, ladder, and anchor tile sheet for floors 1 through 30, including trims that visually connect the mirror and scale puzzle pieces to the tower structure.

shape.silhouette: A clean 32 px grid sheet of tower floors, trims, ladder runs, wall caps, and anchor plates with art-deco ribs and gold bevels.

specs.material_readability: Collision tiles keep bright top edges and darker undersides so footholds remain obvious during silhouette-heavy scenes.

acceptance_criteria: PNG bounds are exactly 256x192 px and divide into an 8x6 grid of 32x32 tiles.
At least 48 unique tiles are present, including platform tops, wall fills, ladder segments, and decorative anchor plates.
Every walkable tile uses a brighter top edge than its underside to keep footholds readable under low-key lighting.

reference_images.label: gdd_markdown
reference_images.purpose: Grounds puzzle rules, object dimensions, HUD placement, and the Bevy 0.14 technical targets for Umbra Scale.

reference_images.label: moodboard
reference_images.purpose: Grounds the twilight art-deco tower mood, silhouette layering, amber beam contrast, and architectural ornament rhythm.

reference_images.label: umbra_character
reference_images.purpose: Grounds Umbra silhouette proportions, eye-glow placement, and cloak-like shadow contours for player and echo scale checks.
